deep_dive.title: GBC: Gradient-Based Connections for Optimizing Multi-Agent Systems
deep_dive.abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) built on large language models (LLMs) provide a promising framework for solving complex tasks through role specialization and structured interaction. However, their performance is often limited by miscoordination and, more fundamentally, the lack of fine-grained credit assignment across agents. Existing approaches typically rely on coarse-grained feedback, making it difficult to identify which agents or interaction steps are responsible for errors. We propose Gradient-Based Connections (GBC), an approach for fine-grained attribution and optimization of multi-agent systems. GBC models a MAS as a computational graph and introduces gradient-based connection weights to quantify the influence of each agent's output on downstream agents at the token level. By constructing an attribution graph and propagating task-specific loss signals backward, our method enables precise identification of error sources and targeted prompt optimization. We further develop AgentChord, an efficient implementation that leverages prefix-based gradient computation. Experiments on MultiWOZ and τ-bench show that GBC improves multi-agent performance and outperforms strong single-agent and multi-agent baselines, and higher attribution quality is associated with greater optimization effectiveness. Code is available at: https://github.com/yxc-cyber/AgentChord.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: gradient-based method for optimizing multi-agent systems, directly applicable to medical diagnosis MAS
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于梯度的多智能体系统优化方法，可直接应用于医学诊断多智能体系统
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于梯度的多智能体系统优化方法，可直接应用于医学诊断多智能体系统
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于梯度的多智能体系统优化方法，可直接应用于医学诊断多智能体系统
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces Gradient-Based Connections (GBC) for fine-grained credit assignment in LLM-based multi-agent systems. Quantifies each agent's influence via gradient weights, enabling structured optimization. Highly transferable to medical multi-agent diagnosis systems.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对大型语言模型构建的多智能体系统中协调不佳和缺乏细粒度信用分配的问题，提出基于梯度连接（GBC）的方法。将多智能体系统建模为计算图，通过梯度权重量化每个智能体对下游任务的影响，实现结构化优化。该方法通用性强，可直接用于医学诊断多智能体系统，如眼底病多智能体诊断，提升各智能体的协作效率。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对大型语言模型构建的多智能体系统中协调不佳和缺乏细粒度信用分配的问题，提出基于梯度连接（GBC）的方法。将多智能体系统建模为计算图，通过梯度权重量化每个智能体对下游任务的影响，实现结构化优化。该方法通用性强，可直接用于医学诊断多智能体系统，如眼底病多智能体诊断，提升各智能体的协作效率。

quick_skim.title: Rethinking Prototype-based Similarity Learning for Few-Shot Object Detection
quick_skim.abstract: Few-shot object detection aims to detect novel object categories from only a few labeled examples, avoiding costly large-scale annotation. Recent prototype-based similarity learning approaches enable training-free adaptation by matching query features with class prototypes. However, they suffer from two fundamental limitations: (i) class confusion arising from inter-class similarity margin collapse, and (ii) insufficient visual cues for precise localization, as similarity scores capture only class-level semantic affinity while providing limited spatial information. To address these issues, we introduce two complementary components. Text-Anchored Semantic Mask (TSMa) leverages class-level text features as semantic anchors to identify semantically aligned channels through channel-wise interaction between visual and text features. By suppressing style-induced spurious responses and emphasizing class-intrinsic signals, TSMa enlarges inter-class similarity margins and mitigates class confusion. We further propose Stage-Aligned Hierarchical Autoregressive Regression (SHARe), which reformulates localization as a hierarchical autoregressive process that progressively refines bounding boxes across multiple stages. SHARe leverages the layer-wise characteristics of ViT representations by aligning feature abstraction levels with regression stages: deeper layers guide early coarse localization, while shallower layers rich in edge and texture cues refine spatial details in later stages. Experiments on COCO demonstrate a new state of the art, outperforming the previous best by +10.1 nAP, with extensive analysis validating each component. The code is available at https://github.com/VisualScienceLab-KHU/ReSet.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: few-shot object detection method with prototype-based similarity learning, transferable to medical image classification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 小样本目标检测方法，基于原型相似性学习，可迁移至医学图像分类
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 小样本目标检测方法，基于原型相似性学习，可迁移至医学图像分类
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 小样本目标检测方法，基于原型相似性学习，可迁移至医学图像分类
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper improves few-shot object detection by addressing class confusion and insufficient spatial cues, introducing Text-Anchored Semantic Mask and other components.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对小样本目标检测中类别混淆和空间信息不足问题，提出文本锚定语义掩码（TSMa）等组件，利用文本特征作为语义锚点增强原型区分性，并改进定位。该方法可迁移至医学图像小样本分类任务，如眼底病分类中的少样本学习。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文针对小样本目标检测中类别混淆和空间信息不足问题，提出文本锚定语义掩码（TSMa）等组件，利用文本特征作为语义锚点增强原型区分性，并改进定位。该方法可迁移至医学图像小样本分类任务，如眼底病分类中的少样本学习。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: few-shot learning for medical image classification

quick_skim.title: Two-Stage Cross-Domain Cervical Abnormality Screening with Cytopathological Image Synthesis and Knowledge Distillation
quick_skim.abstract: Cross-domain diagnosis remains a major challenge in cervical cell pathology due to pronounced domain shifts across institutions and the subtle visual differences among disease stages, which jointly impair model generalization. To address these issues, this paper proposes a two-stage framework for cross-domain cervical cell detection. In the first stage, we propose the Spatially-Continuous Unpaired Neural Schrödinger Bridge (SC-UNSB), which constructs a synthetic intermediate domain to mitigate cross-domain distribution shifts by modeling image translation as an entropy-regularized optimal transport process. In the second stage, we propose a dual-level feature alignment strategy within a knowledge distillation, which progressively aligns shallow structural features and deep semantic representations to facilitate the transfer of domain-invariant knowledge from the source to the target model. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method effectively mitigates domain shift and category ambiguity, improving the cross-domain detection performance.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: cross-domain cervical screening with image synthesis and knowledge distillation, transferable to fundus domain adaptation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 跨域宫颈筛查中的图像合成与知识蒸馏，可迁移至眼底域适应
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 跨域宫颈筛查中的图像合成与知识蒸馏，可迁移至眼底域适应
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 跨域宫颈筛查中的图像合成与知识蒸馏，可迁移至眼底域适应
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a two-stage cross-domain framework using Neural Schrödinger Bridge for image synthesis and knowledge distillation for feature alignment, applicable to domain adaptation in medical imaging.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对跨领域诊断中域偏移和疾病阶段细微差异的问题，提出两阶段框架：第一阶段利用神经薛定谔桥进行无配对图像合成构建中间域，第二阶段通过知识蒸馏实现双层级特征对齐，在宫颈细胞学跨域检测任务上提升了泛化能力，该方法可迁移至眼底疾病跨域诊断。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对跨领域诊断中域偏移和疾病阶段细微差异的问题，提出两阶段框架：第一阶段利用神经薛定谔桥进行无配对图像合成构建中间域，第二阶段通过知识蒸馏实现双层级特征对齐，在宫颈细胞学跨域检测任务上提升了泛化能力，该方法可迁移至眼底疾病跨域诊断。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 眼底病分类与强化学习结合，跨领域迁移方法, especially work that connects or combines: reinforcement learning for medical diagnosis; domain adaptation for fundus image analysis; fundus disease classification using machine learning; policy transfer between different tasks; multi-task learning for fundus disease and RL; application of reinforcement learning to medical imaging; How to apply reinforcement learning to retinal disease diagnosis?; Transfer reinforcement learning methods from other domains to ophthalmology; Domain adaptation for retinal disease treatment planning using reinforcement learning; How to combine reinforcement learning with fundus disease classification.
